threats.title: BlackGovernor.clock() Returns Zero Breaking All Governance Functionality
threats.hypothesis_description: BlackGovernor.clock() has an empty implementation returning default uint48(0). This function is used by the inherited OpenZeppelin Governor framework for proposal snapshot timing, voting period calculations, and EIP-6372 compliance. Returning 0 means all proposal snapshots reference timestamp 0, causing getPastVotes lookups to return 0 for all voters, making it impossible to reach quorum on any proposal. Similarly, CLOCK_MODE() returns an empty string, violating EIP-6372. This renders the entire governance system (BlackGovernor → nudge → tailEmissionRate adjustment) permanently non-functional.
threats.investigation_rationale: The governance system is the sole mechanism to adjust tail emission rates via nudge(). If governance cannot execute, the protocol loses its only inflation control mechanism once tail emissions begin.

proof_of_signal.pattern: function clock() public view override returns (uint48) {} — empty body returns 0; function CLOCK_MODE() public view override returns (string memory) {} — empty body returns empty string

preconditions: BlackGovernor is deployed (always true post-deployment)
Any user attempts to create or vote on a governance proposal

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: clock() returns 0, meaning all proposal snapshots use timestamp 0 for voting power lookups
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: No admin action can fix this — requires contract redeployment or upgrade
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Permanent — affects all proposals from deployment onward

operational_questions: Does the inherited L2Governor use clock() for snapshot calculations?
Can proposals be created at all with clock() returning 0?
Is there a fallback to block.number in the L2Governor implementation?
validation_requirements: Attempt to create a proposal and verify it succeeds or fails
Check if getPastVotes at timestamp 0 returns any meaningful value
Verify if voting period calculations produce valid ranges with clock()=0

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Observe that governance proposals cannot reach quorum because voting power lookups at timestamp 0 return 0
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Protocol enters tail emission phase with no mechanism to adjust emission rate
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: tailEmissionRate remains at MAX_BPS (100%) permanently if nudge() can never be called via governance
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Protocol inflation schedule cannot be adjusted, violating economic design
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: None — this is a passive defect affecting all governance participants

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Complete governance failure — no proposals can pass
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Tail emission rate control permanently broken
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All governance-controlled parameters including tail emission nudge

execution_requirements_analysis.governance_tokens_needed: None — this is a code defect, not an attack

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Attempt to create and vote on a proposal on testnet/mainnet

threats.title: Bribe.withdraw Silent Failure Causes Permanent Accounting Desynchronization
threats.hypothesis_description: Bribe.withdraw() silently skips the withdrawal when amount > balanceOf[tokenId] due to an if-guard instead of a revert. When the Voter contract calls withdraw with an amount exceeding the bribe's tracked balance (possible due to veNFT balance recalculations, poke operations, or rounding), the Bribe's totalSupply and balanceOf remain unchanged. The Voter believes the withdrawal succeeded, but the Bribe retains phantom voting power. This permanently inflates totalSupply relative to actual deposits, diluting all legitimate voters' bribe rewards. The affected tokenId continues earning rewards despite having no active votes.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a fundamental accounting invariant violation. The supply conservation law (totalSupply == sum(balanceOf)) can be permanently broken, causing economic losses for all bribe participants.

proof_of_signal.pattern: if (amount <= balanceOf[tokenId]) { totalSupply -= amount; ... } — silent no-op when condition fails, no revert

preconditions: Voter contract calls bribe.withdraw(amount, tokenId) where amount > balanceOf[tokenId]
This can occur when veNFT voting power changes between deposit and withdrawal (e.g., lock decay, poke recalculation)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: withdraw silently fails, leaving phantom balanceOf and inflated totalSupply
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: No admin recovery mechanism exists for desynchronized bribe state
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Permanent once desync occurs — affected tokenId earns rewards indefinitely

operational_questions: Does the Voter contract ever call withdraw with an amount different from the original deposit?
How does veNFT voting power recalculation (poke) affect deposit/withdraw amounts?
Can a user intentionally trigger this by manipulating their veNFT between vote and reset?
validation_requirements: Trace Voter.reset flow to determine if withdraw amount can exceed deposited amount
Test with veNFT whose voting power changes between vote() and reset()
Verify totalSupply drift after multiple vote/reset cycles

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: User locks tokens and votes, depositing voting power X into bribe
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: User's veNFT voting power changes (lock extends, deposits more, or poke recalculates)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: User resets vote — Voter calls withdraw with recalculated amount Y > X
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Bribe.withdraw silently fails, balanceOf[tokenId] = X remains, totalSupply unchanged
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Single user can trigger this through normal protocol usage

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: No admin fix possible — state is permanently corrupted
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: All bribe reward calculations become incorrect for the affected bribe contract
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Requires redeployment of affected bribe contracts and migration
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All bribe reward distributions for affected gauges

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Any time veNFT balance changes between vote and reset

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Compare totalSupply vs sum(balanceOf) across all tokenIds per bribe
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: TokenIds earning bribe rewards with 0 active votes
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Users who vote, extend locks, then reset

threats.title: MinterUpgradeable.calculate_rebase Arithmetic Underflow Freezes All Protocol Emissions
threats.hypothesis_description: calculate_rebase double-counts smNFT locked balances. veBlackSupply = _veTotal + _smNFTBalance + _superMassiveBonus where _veTotal (balanceOf(ve)) already includes smNFT tokens locked in the ve contract. blackSupply = _blackTotal + _superMassiveBonus. The subtraction circulatingBlack = blackSupply - veBlackSupply underflows in Solidity 0.8 if _veTotal + _smNFTBalance > _blackTotal. As smNFT adoption grows, this condition becomes increasingly likely, causing update_period() to revert permanently and freezing ALL protocol emissions.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a ticking time bomb that becomes more likely as the protocol succeeds. SmNFTs are designed to accumulate over time, and the double-counting error means the protocol has a hard ceiling on smNFT adoption before emissions freeze.

proof_of_signal.pattern: veBlackSupply = _veTotal + _smNFTBalance + _superMassiveBonus; blackSupply = _blackTotal + _superMassiveBonus; circulatingBlack = blackSupply - veBlackSupply — double-counts smNFT balance

preconditions: smNFT balance grows large enough that _veTotal + _smNFTBalance > _blackTotal
_veTotal already includes smNFT locked tokens since they're held by the ve contract

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: calculate_rebase reads ve.smNFTBalance() which returns tokens ALREADY included in black.balanceOf(ve)
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: No admin can fix this without upgrading MinterUpgradeable
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Grows over time as smNFT adoption increases

operational_questions: What is the current ratio of smNFTBalance to totalSupply?
Is smNFTBalance included in black.balanceOf(address(ve))?
How close is the protocol to the underflow threshold?
validation_requirements: Calculate: _veTotal + smNFTBalance vs _blackTotal to measure proximity to underflow
Test calculate_rebase with production-realistic smNFT balances
Verify that smNFT locked tokens ARE counted in ve contract's BLACK balance

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Attacker creates many smNFTs, locking large amounts of BLACK
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: smNFTBalance grows, pushing veBlackSupply past blackSupply
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Next call to update_period() reverts on arithmetic underflow in calculate_rebase
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: All emissions, fee distributions, and epoch transitions are permanently frozen
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Requires accumulating sufficient smNFT balance — could occur naturally or be accelerated

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Complete protocol freeze — no emissions, no distributions
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: All gauges stop receiving rewards; veNFT holders cannot earn rebases
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: update_period, distributeAll, distributeFees — entire emission pipeline

execution_requirements_analysis.governance_tokens_needed: Sufficient BLACK to create smNFTs that push past threshold
execution_requirements_analysis.coordination_complexity: Medium — requires significant capital or time
execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Trigger occurs naturally as smNFT adoption grows

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Monitor smNFTBalance / totalSupply ratio trending toward 1.0

threats.title: BribeFactoryV3.recoverERC20AndUpdateData Calls Wrong Bribe Function Causing Insolvency
threats.hypothesis_description: BribeFactoryV3.recoverERC20AndUpdateData is intended to withdraw tokens from bribes AND update their internal tokenRewardsPerEpoch accounting. However, the implementation calls IBribe.emergencyRecoverERC20() instead of IBribe.recoverERC20AndUpdateData(). This means tokens are withdrawn but the bribe's reward accounting (tokenRewardsPerEpoch) is NOT updated. The bribe contract will then report rewards to users that no longer exist in the contract, causing later claimers' transactions to revert due to insufficient balance — effectively a bribe insolvency event.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a copy-paste bug with direct financial consequences. When admin uses the 'safe' recovery function expecting accounting to be preserved, they unknowingly create an insolvency condition.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Function named recoverERC20AndUpdateData but calls IBribe(_bribe[i]).emergencyRecoverERC20(_tokens[i], _amounts[i]) instead of IBribe(_bribe[i]).recoverERC20AndUpdateData(_tokens[i], _amounts[i])

preconditions: Admin calls BribeFactoryV3.recoverERC20AndUpdateData to safely recover tokens
Bribe contract has active reward tokens for current/future epochs

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Function body is identical to recoverERC20From — wrong function called on bribes
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Admin action triggers the bug unknowingly
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Every time the 'safe' recovery function is used

operational_questions: Has this function been called on-chain yet?
Are there bribes with inflated tokenRewardsPerEpoch from past recoveries?
Are any users unable to claim bribe rewards due to insufficient balance?
validation_requirements: Compare function bodies of recoverERC20From and recoverERC20AndUpdateData — they're identical
Test: call recoverERC20AndUpdateData, verify tokenRewardsPerEpoch is NOT updated
Verify subsequent getReward calls fail for users with earned rewards

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Admin notices issue in a bribe and calls recoverERC20AndUpdateData to safely recover tokens
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Tokens are withdrawn but tokenRewardsPerEpoch remains unchanged
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Users call getReward → earned() calculates rewards based on stale tokenRewardsPerEpoch
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Early claimers drain remaining balance; later claimers' transactions revert
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: None — triggered by normal admin operations

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Admin recovery tool is broken — creates worse problems than it solves
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: No recovery possible without manual intervention on each affected bribe
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All bribe reward claims for affected bribes

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Past calls to recoverERC20AndUpdateData on BribeFactory
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Bribe contracts where token balance < sum of tokenRewardsPerEpoch

threats.title: BlackGovernor.quorum Uses Non-Snapshot smNFT Supply Enabling Quorum Manipulation
threats.hypothesis_description: BlackGovernor.quorum() ignores the blockTimestamp parameter and calls token.getsmNFTPastTotalSupply() without a timestamp argument, returning the CURRENT smNFT total supply rather than the supply at the proposal's snapshot time. This means quorum requirements change in real-time between proposal creation and vote finalization. An attacker can create smNFTs after a proposal is submitted to inflate the quorum threshold, making legitimate proposals fail, or burn/unlock smNFTs to lower the quorum for their own proposals.
threats.investigation_rationale: Quorum manipulation directly undermines governance integrity. The tail emission rate adjustment is the only governance-controlled economic parameter, and this vulnerability allows blocking or forcing nudge proposals.

proof_of_signal.pattern: return (token.getsmNFTPastTotalSupply() * quorumNumerator()) / quorumDenominator() — getsmNFTPastTotalSupply() takes no timestamp parameter, returns current supply not historical

preconditions: Governance proposal is created
Attacker has ability to create or influence smNFT creation between proposal and vote

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: quorum(blockTimestamp) ignores blockTimestamp entirely, uses current supply
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: No admin can fix without redeploying BlackGovernor
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Between proposal creation and vote finalization (votingPeriod = 30 minutes)

operational_questions: Does getsmNFTPastTotalSupply() return current or historical supply?
How volatile is smNFT total supply during a typical voting period?
Can smNFTs be rapidly created/destroyed to swing quorum?
validation_requirements: Verify getsmNFTPastTotalSupply() has no timestamp parameter in IBlackHoleVotes
Test quorum value changes when smNFTs are created during active proposal
Compare with standard Governor quorum which uses snapshot-based supply

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Monitor for governance proposals being submitted
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Create additional smNFTs to inflate getsmNFTPastTotalSupply()
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Quorum threshold increases, causing legitimate proposal to fail
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Alternatively: reduce smNFT supply before submitting attacker's proposal to lower quorum
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Requires sufficient capital to create/destroy smNFTs

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Governance quorum is manipulable, allowing proposal censorship
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Legitimate proposals can be blocked; attacker proposals can be forced through
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All governance proposals including nudge emission rate adjustments

execution_requirements_analysis.governance_tokens_needed: Enough to create smNFTs that meaningfully impact total supply
execution_requirements_analysis.technical_skills: Moderate — understanding of smNFT creation mechanics
execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: During active voting period of target proposal

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: SmNFT creation/destruction events during active voting periods
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Proposals failing quorum despite high participation
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: SmNFT creation spikes correlated with governance activity

threats.title: Missing Bribe.addReward Function Breaks All BribeFactory Reward Management
threats.hypothesis_description: BribeFactoryV3 defines a local IBribe interface that includes addReward(address), and multiple admin functions call this method: addRewardToBribe, addRewardsToBribe, addRewardToBribes, addRewardsToBribes. However, the actual Bribe contract (Bribes.sol) does NOT implement an addReward() function. All calls from BribeFactory to add reward tokens to bribes will revert. The only way to add new reward tokens is through notifyRewardAmount with a connector token, bypassing the intended admin workflow entirely.
threats.investigation_rationale: This is a critical interface mismatch that renders the entire reward token management admin workflow non-functional. Admin cannot pre-register reward tokens on bribes.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Local IBribe interface declares addReward(address) but Bribes.sol has no such function — all addReward calls will revert with missing function selector

preconditions: Admin attempts to add a reward token to any bribe via BribeFactory
Bribe contract is the actual Bribes.sol implementation

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: BribeFactory interface expects addReward but Bribe contract doesn't implement it
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Admin reward management is completely broken

operational_questions: Has any addReward call been attempted on-chain?
Are there bribe contracts that need additional reward tokens added?
Is there an alternative mechanism to register reward tokens?
validation_requirements: Search Bribes.sol for any function matching addReward selector
Attempt to call addRewardToBribe on a deployed bribe and verify revert
Verify notifyRewardAmount auto-adds connector tokens as workaround

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Admin tries to add a new reward token to an existing bribe via BribeFactory
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Transaction reverts because Bribe contract has no addReward function
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Admin workflow for reward token management is completely non-functional
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Only connector tokens can be added to bribes (via notifyRewardAmount auto-add)

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Reward token management admin functions are dead code
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Cannot pre-register non-connector reward tokens on bribes
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Requires Bribe contract upgrade or workaround via notifyRewardAmount

threats.title: Emissions Permanently Locked When Voter totalWeight is Zero
threats.hypothesis_description: GaugeManager.notifyRewardAmount transfers BLACK tokens from the Minter then calculates _ratio = amount * 1e18 / totalWeight. When IVoter(voter).totalWeight() returns 0 (no votes cast), _ratio is 0, index is not incremented, and the transferred tokens become permanently stuck in GaugeManager with no recovery mechanism. There is no sweep function, no fallback distribution, and no revert to prevent the transfer when weight is zero. These tokens represent an entire epoch's gauge emissions that are irrecoverably lost.
threats.investigation_rationale: This represents a permanent loss of protocol emissions. While totalWeight=0 may seem unlikely, it can occur during protocol bootstrap, mass vote resets before epoch flip, or coordinated manipulation.

proof_of_signal.pattern: IERC20Upgradeable(base).safeTransferFrom(msg.sender, address(this), amount); executed before totalWeight check — tokens transferred even if totalWeight==0, index unchanged, no recovery

preconditions: IVoter(voter).totalWeight() == 0 at the moment notifyRewardAmount is called
Minter calls update_period which calls notifyRewardAmount with amount > 0

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Tokens transferred before weight validation; no revert when weight is zero
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: No admin recovery function exists to extract stuck tokens
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any epoch where totalWeight is 0 at the moment of distribution

operational_questions: Has totalWeight ever been 0 during an epoch flip?
Can mass vote resets be coordinated to achieve totalWeight=0?
What is the cost of manipulating totalWeight to 0 vs. the value of trapped emissions?
validation_requirements: Monitor totalWeight at epoch boundaries
Test notifyRewardAmount with totalWeight=0 and verify tokens are stuck
Verify no sweep/recovery function exists in GaugeManager

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Coordinate mass vote resets across veNFT holders before epoch flip
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: With totalWeight=0, _ratio=0, index unchanged, tokens permanently stuck
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Requires majority of voting power to reset — high coordination cost

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Emissions permanently reduced by lost amount
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Gauge emissions for the affected epoch

execution_requirements_analysis.governance_tokens_needed: Majority of voting power to force totalWeight to 0
execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Must achieve totalWeight=0 before epoch flip

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: GaugeManager BLACK balance exceeding expected amounts
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Epochs where index did not increase despite notifyRewardAmount call
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Mass vote resets near epoch boundaries

threats.title: BribeFactoryV3.addRewardsToBribes Loop Indexing Bug Causes Reverts or Wrong Token Additions
threats.hypothesis_description: BribeFactoryV3.addRewardsToBribes has a nested loop bug. The inner loop iterates `k < _token.length` (outer array length) but accesses `_token[i][k]`. If the inner arrays have different lengths from the outer array, this causes out-of-bounds reverts. If inner arrays happen to match the outer length but contain different tokens than intended, wrong reward tokens are added to bribes. This function is completely unusable for its intended purpose in most input configurations.
threats.investigation_rationale: Admin function for batch reward token management is broken, preventing efficient bribe administration.

proof_of_signal.pattern: for(k = 0; k < _token.length; k++){ IBribe(_br).addReward(_token[i][k]); } — inner loop bound is _token.length (outer) not _token[i].length (inner)

preconditions: Admin calls addRewardsToBribes with _token[][] and __bribes[]
Inner arrays of _token have different lengths from _token.length

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Inner loop uses outer array length as bound while accessing inner array elements

operational_questions: Has this function ever been called successfully?
What inputs would the admin typically provide?
Does the addReward revert compound this issue (see OPS-H006)?
validation_requirements: Call addRewardsToBribes with typical inputs and verify revert
Test with pathological inputs to confirm indexing bug
Note: even if indexing were correct, addReward doesn't exist on Bribe (see OPS-H006)

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Admin attempts batch reward token addition
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Function reverts due to out-of-bounds array access or adds wrong tokens
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Admin forced to use individual calls, increasing operational overhead
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Combined with OPS-H006, no path to add rewards exists

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Cannot efficiently manage reward tokens across bribes

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Admin forced to use single-bribe functions

threats.title: GaugeFactoryCL Requires Pre-Funding With Reward Tokens for CL Gauge Creation
threats.hypothesis_description: GaugeFactoryCL.createEternalFarming approves and transfers 1e10 reward tokens to AlgebraEternalFarming via addRewards. However, the factory contract never receives these tokens — no transfer to the factory occurs before the approval. If the factory's balance of rewardToken < 1e10, the safeApprove succeeds but addRewards will fail (or the farming system will pull tokens that don't exist). This blocks ALL CL gauge creation. No deployment script funds the factory with reward tokens.
threats.investigation_rationale: CL gauge creation is a core protocol operation. If the factory cannot create eternal farming incentives, no concentrated liquidity gauges can be deployed.
deployment_assumptions: GaugeFactoryCL must be pre-funded with sufficient reward tokens (at least 1e10 per gauge creation)
threats.config_evidence: No deployment script transfers reward tokens to GaugeFactoryCL. deployment-flows/scripts/cl/ directory has no funding script for the factory.

proof_of_signal.pattern: IERC20(_rewardToken).safeApprove(_algebraEternalFarming, reward); then algebraEternalFarming.addRewards(incentivekey, uint128(reward), 0); — factory approves tokens it doesn't hold

preconditions: GaugeFactoryCL has less than 1e10 reward tokens in its balance
Anyone calls GaugeManager.createGauge with gaugeType=1 (CL)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Factory approves and adds rewards without having the tokens
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Factory must be manually funded before each batch of gauge creations
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time factory balance is insufficient

operational_questions: What is the current reward token balance of GaugeFactoryCL?
Has any CL gauge been successfully created?
Is there an undocumented funding step in the deployment process?
validation_requirements: Check GaugeFactoryCL balance of the BLACK token
Attempt CL gauge creation when factory balance < 1e10
Review all deployment scripts for factory funding

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Wait until factory's reward token balance is exhausted
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: All CL gauge creation attempts revert
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: No new concentrated liquidity pools can be incentivized
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Protocol CL expansion halts until factory is manually refunded
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: None — natural exhaustion of factory balance

potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Simple — transfer reward tokens to factory

threats.title: GaugeCL.setInternalBribe Meaningless Validation Allows Setting address(0) Blocking Fee Distribution
threats.hypothesis_description: GaugeCL.setInternalBribe uses `require(_int >= address(0), 'zero')` which is always true since all addresses are >= address(0) in unsigned arithmetic. This allows the owner to set internal_bribe to address(0). When _claimFees subsequently executes, it calls IERC20.safeApprove(address(0), ...) or IBribe(address(0)).notifyRewardAmount(), both of which revert, permanently blocking fee distribution for the affected CL gauge.
threats.investigation_rationale: Fee distribution is a core protocol function. A single misconfiguration can permanently break it for a gauge with no self-healing mechanism.

proof_of_signal.pattern: require(_int >= address(0), 'zero') — this comparison is always true; address(0) is the minimum possible address value

preconditions: Owner (GaugeFactoryCL via onlyAllowed) calls setInternalBribe with address(0)
Subsequently, _claimFees is called on the gauge

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Validation is a tautology — any address passes the check
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires owner to make the misconfiguration
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Permanent once internal_bribe is set to address(0)

operational_questions: Has setInternalBribe been called with address(0) on any gauge?
Are there other paths to set internal_bribe to address(0)?
Can GaugeManager.setInternalBribeFor also trigger this (it has its own code length check)?
validation_requirements: Verify require(_int >= address(0)) is always true
Test _claimFees with internal_bribe = address(0)
Check GaugeManager.setInternalBribeFor has code length check as mitigation

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Check passes — internal_bribe set to address(0)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Next _claimFees call reverts when trying to interact with address(0)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Fee distribution permanently blocked for this gauge
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Requires owner action — accidental or intentional

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Single gauge fee distribution permanently broken
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Voters for affected gauge lose fee rewards
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Owner must call setInternalBribe with valid address
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Fee distribution for affected CL gauge

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Failed claimFees transactions on specific gauges

threats.title: GaugeFactoryCL.setReferralFee Has No Upper Bound Enabling Complete Fee Capture or DoS
threats.hypothesis_description: GaugeFactoryCL.setReferralFee allows GAUGE_ADMIN or owner to set dibsPercentage to any uint256 value with no upper bound check. Since REFERRAL_FEE_DENOMINATOR is 1000, setting dibsPercentage to 1000 routes 100% of all CL gauge fees to the dibs address, leaving nothing for voters. Setting it above 1000 causes arithmetic underflow in GaugeCL._claimFees (claimed0 -= _dibsFeeToken0 where _dibsFeeToken0 > claimed0), reverting all fee distributions across ALL CL gauges permanently.
threats.investigation_rationale: A single admin parameter change can permanently break fee distribution for all CL gauges or redirect all fees to a chosen address.
threats.config_evidence: deployment-flows/scripts/cl/setReferralFeeInGaugeFactory.js sets it to 500 (50%), which is within valid range but high

proof_of_signal.pattern: function setReferralFee(uint256 _dibsFee) external onlyAllowed { dibsPercentage = _dibsFee; } — no require(_dibsFee < 1000)

preconditions: GAUGE_ADMIN or owner calls setReferralFee with value >= 1000
Any CL gauge subsequently tries to distribute fees via _claimFees

initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Immediate and global — affects all CL gauges

operational_questions: What is the current dibsPercentage value on-chain?
Who holds the GAUGE_ADMIN role?
Is there any governance constraint on fee parameter changes?
validation_requirements: Verify setReferralFee allows values >= 1000
Test _claimFees with dibsPercentage = 1001 and verify underflow revert
Check current deployment value matches deployment script (500)

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: All subsequent _claimFees calls on all CL gauges revert due to underflow
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Fee distribution for ALL CL gauges is permanently blocked
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Fees accumulate in gauges with no way to distribute them
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Requires GAUGE_ADMIN key compromise or malicious admin

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: All CL gauge fee distribution blocked with single parameter change
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: No voter earns fees from any CL pool
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Admin must set dibsPercentage back to valid value, but accumulated fees during outage may have issues
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Fee distribution for all CL gauges
